description_long: This bungalow features a large, sloped gable roof clad in reddish-brown tiles, extending over the entire structure and providing ample coverage. The building is single-storied, with a rectangular footprint and a modest elevation above the ground, supported by several visible columns along one side. The front section includes a partially covered porch area with a white platform, which is slightly elevated and supported by columns. The exterior walls are finished in a neutral, textured material, likely stucco or brick, with minimal decorative elements. The roof overhangs the walls, offering shade and protection from the elements. Windows and doors are limited or not clearly visible, suggesting a simple, functional design typical of suburban or rural bungalows.
description: A single-story bungalow with a large reddish-brown gable roof, elevated porch, and simple rectangular structure supported by columns.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the extended gable roof, partial porch area, and some interior structural details.
description_view_1: Left-side view highlighting the long roof, row of supporting columns, and the full length of the porch platform.
description_view_2: Rear-left view displaying the back wall, roof overhang, and absence of porch or visible openings.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the plain back wall and the uninterrupted expanse of the gable roof.
